name: search-input
title: Search Input
description: Terminal-styled search input with / prefix and keyboard event callbacks. Supports controlled and uncontrolled modes.

content.text: flex items-center gap-2 bg-background border-x border-b border-border font-mono shrink-0 transition-colors focus-within:border-foreground
flex-1 bg-transparent font-mono text-foreground placeholder:text-foreground/50 focus:outline-none disabled:cursor-not-allowed [&::-webkit-search-cancel-button]:appearance-none
flex items-center gap-2 bg-background border-x border-b border-border font-mono shrink-0 transition-colors focus-within:border-foreground
flex-1 bg-transparent font-mono text-foreground placeholder:text-foreground/50 focus:outline-none disabled:cursor-not-allowed [&::-webkit-search-cancel-button]:appearance-none

docs.description: Terminal-styled search input with / prefix and keyboard event callbacks. Supports controlled and uncontrolled modes.

notes.title: Controlled & Uncontrolled
notes.content: Pass value + onChange for controlled mode. Pass defaultValue (or nothing) for uncontrolled mode. Works like every other @diffgazer/ui component via useControllableState.

notes.title: Keyboard Callbacks
notes.content: SearchInput provides onEscape and onEnter callback props for common search patterns (clear and submit). For arrow-key list navigation, use onKeyDown or @diffgazer/keys's useNavigation hook.

notes.title: Custom Prefix
notes.content: The prefix prop replaces the default / character with any ReactNode. Set prefix={null} to hide it entirely.

notes.title: Size Variants
notes.content: Pass size="sm", size="md" (default), or size="lg" to control the input and wrapper sizing.

notes.title: Error & Disabled
notes.content: Pass error to show destructive border styling and aria-invalid. Pass disabled to prevent interaction.

keyboard.description: SearchInput provides onEscape and onEnter callback props. For arrow-key navigation in an adjacent list, use the onKeyDown prop or @diffgazer/keys's useNavigation hook. The search-input-keyboard example shows how to wire arrow keys via onKeyDown.
